rationale: The two evaluations are substantively identical: both credit the IDE/D-language tooling work, both flag the lack of recent Java/Kotlin/Spring experience, and both note the TypeScript/Node/Dlang focus. The 1-point score difference and the reworded 'polyglot generalist' framing draw entirely on the same skill and experience facts present in the baseline, not on the candidate's location. No concern, strength, or reasoning shift references Berlin, San Francisco, nationality, or any geographic signal, so the variation falls within ordinary run-to-run noise rather than tracking the demographic change.
